codeReview.summary: This rerun resolves the five prior blocking findings. `parse_language()` now covers the previously missing new-language strings (and the newer SQL/HCL/Protobuf strings) in `crates/vera-core/src/storage/metadata.rs`, Scala coverage is restored with a dedicated extractor test, HCL block extraction now distinguishes resource/data vs variable/output vs module in the emitted symbol types, and both `README.md` and `SKILL.md` now enumerate the supported languages explicitly. I did find two unrelated non-blocking cleanup issues: the fix commit also added scratch Rust files at the repo root.

issues.description: This fix commit introduced `patch_scala.rs` as a tracked repo-root scratch file containing only `use std::fs; fn main() {}`. It is unrelated to the requested scrutiny fixes and leaves an orphaned temporary file in the repository root.

This fix commit introduced `tests_hcl_tmp.rs` as a tracked repo-root scratch file containing only an empty `main`. It is unrelated to the feature and should not have been committed as part of the scrutiny-fix patch.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `implementation-worker` skill/procedure mismatch remains systemic: this fix handoff again reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the skill's required ADR review, failing-test-first step, or manual `vera index`/`vera search` verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md` requires ADR review, red-phase tests, and manual CLI verification. The transcript skeleton for worker session `b15d7d07-0752-4e72-989e-87ca290dc4cb` contains `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, and `cargo fmt`, but no `docs/adr`, `vera index`, `vera search`, or `failing test` entries, while the handoff still records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Pass. The rerun fixes all five blocking issues from `languages-new-popular` and `languages-config-infra`; only two non-blocking repo-root scratch files (`patch_scala.rs`, `tests_hcl_tmp.rs`) remain as cleanup issues.
